user_prompt: initialize the serial port for printing messages
declare a buffer identical to `g_mono_buffer`, randomly generate data into it
for the next 10 seconds (when ended, use idle_forever) ...
use `MicGainManager::process` on that buffer over and over again as fast as possible. count how many time this has been done
after the 10 seconds, report how many times the process has been done, and vs how many times it should have been done if it was a realistic 16 kHz stream for 10 seconds
implement the file I am looking at in the IDE
agent_reply: - Creates a `240` sample mono buffer matching `g_mono_buffer`.
- Fills it once with deterministic random PCM.
- Runs `MicGainManager::process()` as fast as possible for 10 seconds.
- Prints actual process count, expected real-time count for 16 kHz / 240-sample buffers, calls/sec, and real-time factor.

user_prompt: draw an example of all fonts, starting at x=5 y=5, one line at a time, once with 1.0f size and once with 2.0f size
if the screen gets filled, pause for 5 seconds, then continue with another screen with even bigger fonts
do 3 screens worth or if you run out of fonts
agent_reply: - Draws each font as a pair of lines:
- If the next pair won’t fit, it waits 5 seconds and continues on the next screen.
- Stops after 3 screens or after font `8`, then idles.

user_prompt: implement `void test_pmic()`, just start the serial port and start dumping data from the PMIC through the M5 library, once per second, with a `millis()` time stamp per line
agent_reply: - Dumps PMIC data once per second with `millis()`
- Prints common M5 power fields: PMIC type, charging state, battery %, battery mV, battery current, VBUS mV, ext/USB output
- Prints AXP192/AXP2101 details when detected: ACIN/VBUS present, ACIN/VBUS voltage/current, charge/discharge current

user_prompt: Guru Meditation Error: Core  0 panic'ed (Cache disabled but cached memory region accessed).
I (1731) esp_core_dump_flash: Save core dump to flash...
E (1736) esp_core_dump_flash: Core dump flash config is corrupted! CRC=0x7bd5c66f instead of 0x0
E (1744) esp_core_dump_elf: Elf write init failed!
E (1749) esp_core_dump_common: Core dump write failed with error=-1
I (1755) esp_core_dump_flash: Core dump has been saved to flash.
got a boot loop here, and the decoder isn't working
agent_reply: I found the boot-loop cause and fixed the likely “decoder isn’t working” part too.

The crash was happening before app startup, inside ESP-IDF flash initialization. The decoded trace pointed at `esp_flash_read_chip_id()` calling `memcpy` while flash cache was disabled. The problem was that `memcpy` had been linked into flash at `0x401f...`, so the CPU faulted with:
`Cache disabled but cached memory region accessed`
I added a small linker override component:
That keeps only the needed libc memory/string routines in IRAM without turning the full PSRAM workaround back on. After a clean rebuild, `memcpy` moved to IRAM:

I also set `CONFIG_SPIRAM_CACHE_LIBMEM_IN_IRAM=y` in `platformio.ini` and `sdkconfig.defaults`, but the linker fragment is the real fix here because that sdkconfig option is gated by the older PSRAM workaround path.
Verification: `pio run -e thefly_bringuptest -j 4` now builds successfully. I did not upload/flash.
One small note: the build regenerated `src/version.c`, and the new `components/thefly_linker_overrides` files are untracked, so those need to be included when you commit this fix.

`Cache disabled but cached memory region accessed`
I added a small linker override component:
That keeps only the needed libc memory/string routines in IRAM without turning the full PSRAM workaround back on. After a clean rebuild, `memcpy` moved to IRAM:

I also set `CONFIG_SPIRAM_CACHE_LIBMEM_IN_IRAM=y` in `platformio.ini` and `sdkconfig.defaults`, but the linker fragment is the real fix here because that sdkconfig option is gated by the older PSRAM workaround path.
Verification: `pio run -e thefly_bringuptest -j 4` now builds successfully. I did not upload/flash.
One small note: the build regenerated `src/version.c`, and the new `components/thefly_linker_overrides` files are untracked, so those need to be included when you commit this fix.

user_prompt: I just did a test with bluetooth, the test went fine, I took out the card to read the file, the file is fine after decoding, both channels
but I forgot to put the card back in, the device is still running
I tried to test again, it gave me a "cannot start recording" error and then went back into the establishing connection waiting screen.

microSD card errors should be fatal, but for that particular error, see if you can do some sort of check to see if the card is plain missing or just full or something, make the error better, but make it fatal
agent_reply: I added a microSD health probe in [MicroSdCard.h](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/MicroSdCard/MicroSdCard.h:13) and [MicroSdCard.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/MicroSdCard/MicroSdCard.cpp:120). It now distinguishes:

Then [AudioFileRecorder.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioFileRecorder/AudioFileRecorder.cpp:103) makes recording-start storage failures fatal. On start it now probes the card before opening the recording file, and if file creation still fails it probes again before choosing the fatal message.
So the case you hit should now stop on a fatal error like:
That avoids the current path where Bluetooth start failure becomes a dismissible error and returns to the waiting screen.
I did not hardware-test the “remove card while device stays powered” case here, so the next useful smoke test is exactly the sequence you just performed.
